repos.brief:
**What it does:** Ponytail is a JavaScript plugin for AI coding agents (Claude Code, Cursor, etc.) that enforces YAGNI-style minimalism, steering agents toward the simplest possible solution—like swapping a flatpickr date picker installation for a native `<input type="date">`.

**Why now:** It gained 19,615 GitHub stars this week, signaling that developers are actively frustrated with AI agents that over-engineer solutions and inflate costs, tokens, and code bloat.

**Build with it:** Drop the ponytail skill into your Claude Code or Cursor setup to measurably cut generated LOC (~54% mean reduction) and agent API costs (~20%) on real feature work—useful immediately if you're building internal tools or prototypes where agent sessions are burning budget.

**What it does:** OpenMontage is an open-source agentic video production system that gives AI coding assistants 12 pipelines and 52 tools to handle research, scripting, asset generation, editing, and final rendering — producing real motion-clip videos, not just animated stills.

**Why now:** The repo gained 15,097 stars this week and hit #1 on GitHub Trending, signaling a breakout moment where developers are actively looking to fork, contribute, and build on top of it.

**Build with it:** Wire OpenMontage into a content automation workflow — feed it a topic, a competitor's video URL, or a product brief and have it output a finished, captioned video for under $2 using Kling, ElevenLabs, or free stock pipelines.

**What it does:** codebase-memory-mcp is a single static binary MCP server that parses any codebase into a persistent SQLite knowledge graph using tree-sitter AST analysis across 158 languages, answering structural queries about functions, classes, call chains, and HTTP routes in under 1ms with zero dependencies.

**Why now:** It gained over 11,000 stars this week and ships with native support for 11 coding agents including Claude Code, Cursor, Windsurf, and Gemini CLI, making it immediately droppable into whatever AI-assisted workflow you already use.

**Build with it:** Wire it into your agent's MCP config to give it instant, token-efficient context about large unfamiliar codebases — particularly useful if you're building agents that need to navigate, refactor, or generate code across multi-service repos without burning context on file-by-file reads.

**What it does:** Agent Reach is a Python CLI and MCP toolkit that gives AI agents (Claude Code, Cursor, Windsurf, etc.) the ability to read and search Twitter, Reddit, YouTube, GitHub, Bilibili, XiaoHongShu, RSS feeds, and arbitrary web pages—all without paid APIs, by routing through free backends and browser cookie reuse.

**Why now:** It hit #1 on GitHub Trending this week with nearly 9,000 new stars, driven by demand for agent tooling that bypasses the paywalls and IP blocks that make real-time web access impractical for most LLM workflows.

**Build with it:** Wire it into your agent as an MCP server and build competitive intelligence pipelines that pull live Twitter sentiment, Reddit bug reports, and YouTube transcripts into a single structured context—no API keys, no scraper maintenance, just `agent-reach doctor` when something breaks.

**What it does:** Headroom is a context compression layer that sits between your AI agent and the LLM, shrinking tool outputs, logs, RAG chunks, and conversation history by 60–95% before they hit the model—with a Python/TypeScript library, drop-in proxy, MCP server, and one-command wrapping for tools like Claude Code, Cursor, and Codex.

**Why now:** Context windows are filling up faster than they're growing as agents run longer multi-step tasks, and this week's 7,817-star spike signals the developer community is hitting that wall hard and actively hunting for solutions.

**Build with it:** Drop `headroom proxy --port 8787` in front of any OpenAI-compatible endpoint to instantly cut token costs and latency on your agent pipeline, or use `headroom wrap claude` to compress Claude Code's context without changing a single line of your own code.

**What it does:** Understand Anything analyzes any codebase with a multi-agent pipeline and renders every file, function, class, and dependency as an interactive knowledge graph you can pan, zoom, search, and query in natural language.

**Why now:** The repo jumped 3,619 stars this week, surfacing alongside the current wave of agentic coding tools — it already ships native integrations for Claude Code, Codex, Gemini CLI, Copilot, and several others you're likely already running.

**Build with it:** Drop it into an onboarding workflow so new engineers can ask "where does authentication live?" and get a visual answer instantly, or wire its graph output to a RAG pipeline to give your AI agent a structured map of a client's codebase before it writes a single line.

**What it does:** Hermes Agent is an open-source, self-improving AI agent from Nous Research that maintains a persistent learning loop — creating and refining skills from experience, modeling the user across sessions, and running across terminal, Telegram, Discord, Slack, and six execution backends including serverless Modal and Daytona.

**Why now:** It gained 6,510 GitHub stars this week, signaling a surge of developer attention likely tied to growing demand for agents that aren't locked to a single model or laptop, and its compatibility with the open agentskills.io standard makes it a timely infrastructure bet.

**Build with it:** Deploy it on a $5 VPS, point it at your own model endpoint via OpenRouter or Nous Portal, then wire up a Telegram interface and cron-scheduled automations to ship a personal AI assistant that gets smarter the more it's used — with trajectory export built in for fine-tuning your own tool-calling model.

**What it does:** AI Website Cloner Template is a Next.js boilerplate that lets you point any AI coding agent at a target URL and reconstruct the full site—design tokens, assets, and component structure—into clean TypeScript with Tailwind and shadcn/ui.

**Why now:** The repo jumped 6,299 stars this week, signaling a surge of builders using it alongside Claude Code's newly released Opus 4.7, which the template explicitly recommends for best cloning results.

**Build with it:** Fork the template, run `/clone-website` against a competitor's landing page or a design inspiration site, then use the generated codebase as a starting point to ship your own product UI without starting from scratch.

**What it does:** AI Berkshire is a Python framework of structured investment research prompts and multi-agent workflows that applies the methodologies of Buffett, Munger, and two prominent Chinese value investors to produce forced, opinionated buy/pass verdicts with price targets—not balanced non-answers.

**Why now:** The repo gained 6,000+ stars this week, signaling strong builder interest in moving beyond generic LLM financial chat toward disciplined, reproducible analysis pipelines with Claude Code and Codex.

**Build with it:** Fork the multi-agent `/investment-team` workflow and extend it with real-time data via MCP connectors—pulling live financials, filings, or earnings transcripts—so each parallel agent grounds its analysis in fresh numbers rather than cached training data.

**What it does:** This repo provides 817 structured cybersecurity skills in a standardized format (agentskills.io), mapped across six frameworks including MITRE ATT&CK and NIST CSF 2.0, so you can drop production-grade security reasoning directly into any AI agent or coding assistant.

**Why now:** It gained nearly 5,000 stars this week, signaling strong community momentum, and its inclusion of the newly added MITRE F3 fraud framework makes it timely for teams building AI agents that need to handle financial threat scenarios.

**Build with it:** Point Claude Code, Cursor, or any MCP-compatible agent at the skill library to give it context-aware judgment for tasks like scoping a cloud breach, generating Sigma detection rules, or running a red-team simulation — without training a custom model.
